raw.objective: “to clarify using population pharmacokinetic analysis whether CYP2C19 polymorphism affects PB pharmacokinetics”
raw.covariates: Tested: WT, CYP2C19 on CL Included: WT, CYP2C19 on CL
raw.observations: Poor metabolisers show a PHB CL reduced by 19.8%. No goodness of fit or other measures to judge the model quality by.
raw.equations: G1=0 if(CYP2C19==11 | CYP2C19==22 | CYP2C19==23†) G1=1 CL/F=theta1*((WT/60)**theta2)*(1+theta3**G1)*(1+eta1) Css=D/(CL*Tau) Cobs=Css*(1+eps1) † if CYP2C19 is one of *1/*1, *2/*2 or *2/*3, the individual is a poor metabolizer Theta1 converted from mL/kg/h as reported in the article to L/h Theta2 converted due to CL conversion

extraction_target.title: CYP2C19 polymorphism effect on phenobarbitone. Pharmacokinetics in Japanese patients with epilepsy: analysis by population pharmacokinetics.

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: G1=0 if(CYP2C19==11 | CYP2C19==22 | CYP2C19==23†) G1=1 CL/F=theta1*((WT/60)**theta2)*(1+theta3**G1)*(1+eta1) Css=D/(CL*Tau) Cobs=Css*(1+eps1) † if CYP2C19 is one of *1/*1, *2/*2 or *2/*3, the individual is a poor metabolizer Theta1 converted from mL/kg/h as reported in the article to L/h Theta2 converted due to CL conversion
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to clarify using population pharmacokinetic analysis whether CYP2C19 polymorphism affects PB pharmacokinetics”

covariates.raw: Tested: WT, CYP2C19 on CL Included: WT, CYP2C19 on CL

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
